baseline.note: Oracle-achievable ceiling: a perfect reviewer reproduces every gold verdict. Re-baseline after Phase 4.5 if a live framework's best stabilises below 100 on the swapped-in interview labels.

cases.desc: The agent's latest judged verdict_accuracy must be >= baseline (minus tolerance).

cases.assert: A perfect-oracle generate scores verdict_accuracy=100% AND source_of_truth_match=100% over all gold cases.
An empty/blank emission scores verdict_accuracy=0% (no saturation trap — empty output is never credited).
A verdict string outside {yes, no, missing-docs} scores 0% (enum-constrained).

On the limit=0 conflict, the corpus ranks cli/products.md (2026-06-25) above reference/products.md (2026-06-10), so source_of_truth=cli/products.md and reference/products.md is listed in other_matches.
The labeled set exercises all three verdict classes: yes (BR-001), no (BR-002 conflict + BR-004 single-match), missing-docs (BR-003).
BR-004 returns 'no' from the documented default (60 min) even though the report's claimed Expected Result (30 min) differs — the agent compares observed to the DOCS, not to the reporter's claim.
